evals.prompt: I've built a React app with a Node.js backend that talks to Postgres. The build succeeds but I don't know if the end-to-end flow actually works. User signs up → gets email confirmation → logs in → sees dashboard. How do I validate this without unit tests?
evals.expected_output: Response should invoke functional-validation skill. Recommend bottom-up: database alive?, API endpoints respond?, frontend loads and can interact?. Point to 4-step protocol (Build → Exercise → Capture → Verdict). Reference fullstack-validation or e2e-validate for multi-layer orchestration. Ask about evidence capture targets (screenshots, response bodies, logs). NO mention of jest/mocha/pytest.

evals.prompt: Our iOS app ships, users complain it crashes on deep links. We have unit tests passing. What evidence should I capture to prove deep links work?
evals.expected_output: Response should invoke functional-validation skill, then ios-validation skill. Emphasize: unit tests didn't catch this (real system failure). Recommend: boot simulator, install actual .app, test actual deep links via xcrun simctl openurl, capture screenshots of landing screens, capture logs. Point to ios-validation skill for 9-step protocol. Mention idb accessibility tree if UI automation needed.
